name: Tri-County Hdc Ltd
description_short: 501(c)(3) nonprofit providing free HUD-certified housing counseling, first-time homebuyer education, and affordable housing development across three Pennsylvania counties since 1990.
description_long:
Tri-County Housing Development Corporation, Inc. was founded in 1990 as a 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization focused on neighborhood revitalization through affordable housing development. Operating across Cumberland, Dauphin, and Perry Counties in Pennsylvania, the organization has developed or rehabilitated over 800 affordable housing units for low- and moderate-income individuals and families. The organization is governed by 18 active volunteer board members and 8 advisory board members, supported by a small staff of 2 full-time and 1 part-time employee.

Tri-County Housing offers multiple free services to the community. Their flagship program is a free, HUD-certified monthly first-time homebuyer class held in-person on Saturdays from 8 a.m. to 4 p.m. The eight-hour course covers mortgages, credit, budgeting, and homeownership fundamentals. Upon completion, participants receive a certificate qualifying them for down payment and closing cost assistance when purchasing their first home. The organization also provides comprehensive housing counseling services as a HUD Certified Housing Counseling Program, established in 2022, designed to enhance financial literacy and expand homeownership opportunities.

Tri-County Housing distinguishes itself through its nonprofit mission, volunteer-driven governance, and integration of both direct housing development and financial education. They provide tangible support beyond counseling—including down payment assistance and closing cost help tied to course completion. The organization has also secured federal funding, including $300,000 from the Federal Home Loan Bank of Pittsburgh for affordable housing initiatives. Their classes include free parking, meals (breakfast pastries and lunch), and aim to pre-qualify participants for Pennsylvania Housing Financing Agency (PHFA) mortgage loans.

The organization is legitimate and well-established with 35 years of operational history. However, prospective homebuyers should note strict attendance policies: failure to cancel with 48 hours' notice results in a three-month restriction from future classes, and late arrivals or no-shows within the past three months result in automatic registration revocation. The classes do not accommodate children during instruction. This is a genuine nonprofit resource best suited for serious first-time homebuyers in the tri-county region who can commit to full-day Saturday attendance.
pros: Free eight-hour first-time homebuyer class with HUD certification, including meals and parking
Certificate of completion qualifies attendees for down payment and closing cost assistance
35-year history since 1990 with 800+ affordable housing units developed or rehabilitated
Free comprehensive housing counseling services through HUD Certified Housing Counseling Program
Volunteer-driven 501(c)(3) nonprofit with community focus on neighborhood revitalization
Pre-qualification support for Pennsylvania Housing Financing Agency (PHFA) mortgage loans
Recent federal funding ($300,000 from Federal Home Loan Bank) demonstrates credibility and resources
cons: Strict 48-hour cancellation policy with three-month restriction for no-shows or late cancellations
Automatic registration revocation if attendee was late or no-show within past three months
Classes held only on Saturdays, limiting accessibility for those with weekend work schedules
No childcare accommodations during class time, which may exclude some single parents or caregivers
Limited staff (2 full-time, 1 part-time employee) may affect responsiveness and capacity
best_for: First-time homebuyers in Cumberland, Dauphin, or Perry Counties, Pennsylvania seeking free education
Low- to moderate-income individuals and families pursuing homeownership with down payment assistance needs
Prospective PHFA mortgage loan applicants requiring HUD-certified pre-qualification counseling
Community members seeking free financial literacy and housing counseling services
diagnosis: Tri-County Housing is best for first-time homebuyers in the tri-county Pennsylvania region who qualify as low- to moderate-income and can commit to full-day Saturday class attendance. The critical caveat is their strict no-show/cancellation policy—missed classes result in a three-month ban with no exceptions, making this suitable only for those who can guarantee availability.
services: Free monthly first-time homebuyer education class (8 hours, Saturdays)
HUD Certified Housing Counseling Program services
Financial literacy and credit education counseling
Closing cost assistance for qualified homebuyers
Affordable housing development and acquisition projects
Certificate issuance for homebuyer course completion
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 201 N Front St, 1St Floor, Harrisburg, PA 17101
meta_description: Pennsylvania nonprofit providing free HUD-certified housing counseling, first-time homebuyer education, and affordable housing development.
